metadata.opportunity: Aurora MySQL to distributed SQL modernization

turns.text: Question one: we rely heavily on MySQL compatibility including JSON columns, stored procedures in legacy services, and transactional semantics.
We can document compatibility by workload slice. For migration, we usually validate SQL features, transaction behavior, and index strategy using sampled production queries.
How do online schema changes behave at 50TB? We cannot absorb prolonged lock risk or late-night cutovers every sprint.
We should benchmark representative DDL operations in the POC and define acceptable impact windows. We will include rollback and guardrail procedures.
For analytics, product managers want near-real-time dashboards. Today we offload delayed extracts to Redshift and freshness is a pain.
Understood. We can evaluate HTAP patterns with a phased approach and measure query latency, freshness, and replication overhead by workload class.
We will send a technical questionnaire: top 20 read queries, top 10 write transactions, ETL schedule, and RPO/RTO goals.
